items.headline: Vegas Golden Knights Anaheim Ducks game 4 recap May 10 2026
items.description: ANAHEIM -- Cutter Gauthier had three assists for the Anaheim Ducks in a 4-3 win against the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round at Honda Center on Sunday.

items.headline: Cole Caufield shines for Canadiens in front of Australian fans
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Cole Caufield needed something, anything, to change his fortunes and get out of his postseason scoring funk.
Who knew it would come from halfway around the world?

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres too emotional in Game 3 loss to Montreal Canadiens
items.description: MONTREAL – Hours before puck drop between the Buffalo Sabres and Montreal Canadiens on Sunday, Lindy Ruff contemplated the insanity the team was anticipating from the Bell Centre crowd, the volume and intensity and emotion. The Sabres coach, a veteran of both playing and coaching in Montreal, knew what might

items.headline: Montreal Canadiens Jakub Dobes continues quick rise during playoffs
items.description: MONTREAL -- Jakub Dobes fought back the tears as he pointed to his heart, then up to the crowd that was chanting his name.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.

items.headline: Start times for Stanley Cup Playoff games on May 14
items.description:
NEW YORK -- The National Hockey League today announced the following updates to the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs Second Round schedule:
Thursday, May 14
1\. The start time for Game 5 of the Second Round series between the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres has been set for 7 p.m. ET

items.headline: Mark Stone injury status update May 10 2026
items.description:
ANAHEIM -- Mark Stone did not play for the Vegas Golden Knights in a 4-3 loss to the Anaheim Ducks in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round at Honda Center on Sunday.

items.headline: Jakub Dobes Montreal Canadiens chants from fans after Game 3
items.description:
Jakub Dobes looked like he was at a loss for words.
After the Montreal Canadiens win over the Buffalo Sabres in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference Second Round on Sunday, fans at the Bell Centre showed their appreciation for the Canadiens netminder, who’s quickly becoming a fan favorite for

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres Montreal Canadiens Game 3 recap May 10 2026
items.description: MONTREAL -- Alex Newhook scored twice for the second straight game, and the Montreal Canadiens scored four straight goals en route to a 6-2 victory against the Buffalo Sabres in Game 3 of the Eastern Conference Second Round at Bell Centre on Sunday.

items.headline: Color of Hockey: Culture thrives at Asian Championship
items.description: William Douglas has been writing The Color of Hockey blog since 2012. Douglas joined NHL.com in 2019 and writes about people of color in the sport. Today, as part of NHL.com's celebration of Asian & Pacific Islander Heritage month, he profiles the Asian Hockey Championship, a three-decade-old tournament and cultural

items.headline: Radko Gudas injury status update May 10 2026
items.description: ANAHEIM -- Radko Gudas did not play for the Anaheim Ducks in Game 4 of the Western Conference Second Round against the Vegas Golden Knights at Honda Center on Sunday.

items.headline: Kirk Muller Montreal Canadiens torch Game 3
items.description:
The last time the Montreal Canadiens won the Stanley Cup, Kirk Muller helped lead the charge. So, it’s only fitting to have him in the building as the team makes another push.

items.description:

items.headline: Avalanche remain confident in goaltending duo entering Game 4 against Wild
items.description:
MINNEAPOLIS -- Jared Bednar has said it from the start of the Stanley Cup Playoffs: the Colorado Avalanche coach is perfectly comfortable switching back and forth between goaltenders Scott Wedgewood and Mackenzie Blackwood.
Now it remains to be seen if the switch is on and if Blackwood gets his first

items.headline: Kirill Kaprizov looks to be game changer for Minnesota Wild in Game 4
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Kirill Kaprizov has the look now, which means the Minnesota Wild have a chance.
